metadata.title: Public-Web Research Profiles - 2026-07-15 Batch 8 I
metadata.notes: Public-web research only. Identity, employer, and legal-rightsholder roles are kept distinct; historical rights are not assigned to later employers. No external communications were made.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Maria Francesca Cardone is a CREA researcher at the Research Centre for Viticulture and Enology. CREA's official plant-IP catalog identifies CREA, not Cardone personally or a later commercialization partner, as the titleholder for the cited CREA-bred grape variety Triviani. Rights for other denominations must remain record-specific unless their titleholder is independently confirmed.
profiles.headquarters: CREA Research Centre for Viticulture and Enology, Via Casamassima 148, 70010 Turi (BA), Italy; CREA central administration is in Rome
profiles.leadershipSummary: Cardone works within a multi-scientist public breeding program. The official catalog and program materials identify a bench including Donato Antonacci, Carlo Bergamini, Lucia Forleo, Rocco Perniola, Antonio Marsico, and Riccardo Velasco, so continuity is institutional rather than dependent on Cardone personally.
profiles.primaryContactTitle: Researcher, CREA Research Centre for Viticulture and Enology

webResearchSources.label: Official CREA researcher profile
Official CREA plant intellectual-property catalog
Official CREA workshop listing and public business contact

profiles.webResearchNotes: The exact-name identity is supported by CREA's official profile and contact listing. The source-backed employer and rights evidence makes this a public-institution employee profile rather than a personal acquisition target. NuVaUT or other commercial partners must not be treated as owners without a cultivar-specific title or assignment record.

profiles.ownershipType: Founder-breeder and personal plant-right holder operating through a family nursery
profiles.ownershipSummary: The exact-name record resolves to Robert W. 'Bob' Gabriel, founder and president of Oregon Blueberry Farms and Nursery. Official Canadian PBR and U.S. patent records name Robert Gabriel personally as breeder and holder or assignee for OBF0604, marketed as MegasBlue. The nursery is the operating and commercialization platform, but it should not replace Gabriel as titleholder where the legal record names him individually.
profiles.headquarters: 8474 Hazelgreen Road NE, Silverton, Oregon 97381, United States
profiles.leadershipSummary: The official team page shows a family-company bench: Bob Gabriel as founder and president, Adam Wagner as breeding manager, Jon Horton in corporate development, Doug McCann in sales, and Bruce Gabriel in nursery operations. This supports operating continuity while leaving cultivar title with the holder named in each record.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: The official company team page identifies Bob Gabriel as founder and president, and the company catalog commercializes Gabriel-bred selections. This establishes company affiliation but is not blanket proof that the company owns patents or PBRs recorded in Gabriel's personal name.

webResearchSources.label: Official Oregon Blueberry team and leadership page
CFIA PBR record for OBF0604 / MegasBlue
U.S. plant patent for OBF0604

profiles.webResearchNotes: Robert Gabriel began blueberry breeding trials in 2002 according to the official company history. Later selections can involve other breeders and assignment patterns, so the Bob Gabriel profile should include only records that name him and should not absorb all Oregon Blueberry breeding output. Registered-symbol uses in a catalog were not treated as trademark ownership evidence.

profiles.ownershipType: Unresolved Korean fruit breeder identity and rightsholder
profiles.ownershipSummary: The romanized exact name and associated peach, apricot, and pear denominations were not sufficient to distinguish a unique individual or legal rightsholder in reliable public sources. No employer, nursery, public institute, or personal ownership has been assigned.
profiles.headquarters: Unresolved; registry geography indicates the Republic of Korea but does not establish a business location
profiles.leadershipSummary: No source-backed employer timeline, management role, succession path, or breeding bench was found for the exact person.

webResearchSources.label: Official Korea Seed and Variety Service portal

profiles.webResearchNotes: The Korean personal name is non-unique, and searches using the romanization, Korean-name candidates, and cultivar denominations did not produce a sufficiently reliable identity match. The official registry authority is provided as the appropriate primary source, but it did not yield a public exact-person profile suitable for assigning ownership or contact details.

profiles.ownershipType: Family-company apple breeder and operating executive with cultivar-specific external assignments
profiles.ownershipSummary: Alexandre Grard is an equal owner of Gradilis Holding and manager of Gradilis Production. Patent and PBR evidence shows that legal title is cultivar- and date-specific: Gradisca moved from members of the Grard family to Gradilis Recherche and then to ABCz Group B.V., which is also shown as the current Canadian holder. The person, family holding company, research subsidiary, and later outside holder must remain separate records.
profiles.headquarters: Mudaison, Herault, France; current holder ABCz Group B.V. is a separate Dutch entity
profiles.leadershipSummary: The official company page identifies Olivier Grard as chief executive, Alexandre Grard as deputy director and Gradilis Production manager, Francois Chevalier as head of Gradilis Recherche, and Thibault Vasseur in nursery leadership. This is a deep family and technical bench, while title succession still must be checked cultivar by cultivar.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Gradilis's official company page identifies Alexandre Grard as an equal owner and operating manager. That affiliation does not prove that the holding company owns every Grard-associated cultivar, particularly where patent or PBR records show a research subsidiary or outside assignee.

webResearchSources.label: Official Gradilis ownership and management page
U.S. Gradisca patent assignment history
CFIA Gradisca PBR record naming current holder

profiles.webResearchNotes: The exact-name identity and current Gradilis role are verified. A record split is mandatory because an aggregated Grard profile otherwise attributes rights held by Gradilis Recherche or ABCz Group B.V. to Alexandre or to a later/current family employer.

profiles.ownershipType: Historical Japanese prefectural research-institute citrus breeder
profiles.ownershipSummary: The identity resolves to Akira Isobe of the Kumamoto prefectural fruit research program. Japan's official variety record names Isobe among the breeders of Hinoasuka, while Kumamoto's official materials treat the Hino and Kumamoto EC varieties as prefecture-bred protected assets. Rights belong to the prefectural program or the titleholder in each record, not to Isobe personally. Only the citrus subset is verified for this identity.
profiles.headquarters: Kumamoto Prefectural Agricultural Research Center, Fruit Tree Research Institute, Uki, Kumamoto Prefecture, Japan
profiles.leadershipSummary: Isobe is a historical public-sector breeder. Kumamoto's current Fruit Tree Research Institute continues citrus and deciduous-fruit selection and breeding, providing institutional continuity, but no evidence supports a personal business, personal rights succession, or current executive role for Isobe.
profiles.primaryContactName: Kumamoto Prefectural Agricultural Research Center, Fruit Tree Research Institute

webResearchSources.label: Official MAFF variety record for Hinoasuka
Official Kumamoto Fruit Tree Research Institute page
Official Kumamoto propagation policy for prefecture-bred protected varieties

profiles.webResearchNotes: The local aggregation contains 19 records, but direct cultivar review supports Isobe only for the 12 citrus records. Five grape and two pear records cannot be assigned to this Akira Isobe and require separate identity resolution. The public-institution status and prefectural ownership make the verified citrus profile non-actionable.

profiles.ownershipType: Independent retired mango breeder and personal rightsholder
profiles.ownershipSummary: Kenneth Rayner is the independent Katherine-based breeder of the Rayner mango selections. Official Australian PBR journals identify Kenneth Rayner as applicant and titleholder for selections including R10/8, RA/17, and RA/36, and the U.S. RA/17 patent identifies him as inventor and individual assignee. These are personal breeder rights rather than rights of a later employer.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Rayner is a retired, late-career independent breeder. Public reporting describes decades of personal breeding work and a scholarship legacy, but no named breeding successor, assignee successor, or operating team has been verified. Any succession analysis should therefore remain tied to current registry title and estate or assignment evidence.
profiles.primaryContactTitle: PBR titleholder; historical official-registry contact, verify currency before use

webResearchSources.label: Official IP Australia PBR journal listing Rayner applications
U.S. patent application for RA/17
Northern Territory Farmers profile of the retired breeder

profiles.webResearchNotes: The public phone is retained only because it appears in an official titleholder journal and may now be stale. Lady Jane is supported as the commercial name for RA/17. No employer or commercial distributor was promoted to owner without a title or assignment record.

profiles.ownershipType: Dutch breeder with historical raspberry-company rights and a later ornamental-breeding affiliation
profiles.ownershipSummary: The name resolves to Andre Smaal. Historical raspberry patents show Smaal as co-inventor and Advanced Berry Breeding B.V. as assignee. An official PBR bulletin records later assignments of Advaberimar, Advabertwee, and Advabereen to Allberry B.V. Smaal's current association with Hydrangea Breeders Association and Agriom is a later ornamental role and does not carry the historical raspberry rights forward.
profiles.headquarters: Current public business location: Achterweg 58A, De Kwakel, Netherlands; historical raspberry activity involved Advanced Berry Breeding and related Dutch entities
profiles.leadershipSummary: The historical raspberry bench included Smaal and co-breeder Peter de Weert, with later group management beyond the founders. Current Hydrangea Breeders Association materials identify Smaal with Jaap Stelder and an Agriom-linked team. The two periods and crop portfolios require separate operational and ownership treatment.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Current public professional and trade-show materials support Smaal's Hydrangea Breeders Association and Agriom affiliation. They do not prove title to his earlier Advanced Berry Breeding raspberry portfolio.

webResearchSources.label: U.S. plant patent for Advaberimar
Official ONSSA PBR bulletin recording assignment to Allberry
IPM Essen exhibitor publication with current HBA business contact

profiles.webResearchNotes: A record split is required across time, crops, and entities. Historical Advanced Berry Breeding inventorship, later Allberry assignments, and current HBA/Agriom affiliation are supported separately; none is treated as blanket ownership of the others' records.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Dan E. Parfitt is Pomologist Emeritus at the University of California, Davis. UC sources state that the University of California patents and licenses its pistachio varieties, so the Regents or the specific university titleholder owns the protected varieties rather than Parfitt personally. Nursery licensees and later program leaders are not historical rightsholders merely by succeeding the breeding work.
profiles.headquarters: Department of Plant Sciences, University of California, Davis, California, United States
profiles.leadershipSummary: Parfitt is emeritus. UC Davis identifies Patrick J. Brown and Giulia Marino as current pistachio-program leaders, supporting a clear institutional bench and succession path. Earlier field and evaluation work also involved UC and cooperative-extension collaborators, including Craig Kallsen.

webResearchSources.label: Official UC Davis profile for Dan Parfitt
Official UC Davis tree-variety licensing program
Official UC Davis pistachio program succession announcement

profiles.webResearchNotes: This is an institutional, non-actionable profile. The cultivar evidence is strong, but ownership and licensing inquiries belong with UC Davis and the Regents, not the retired breeder or licensed nurseries. Only the publicly listed business phone is included; no inferred email is supplied.

profiles.ownershipType: Historical public-university avocado researcher and co-breeder
profiles.ownershipSummary: Fred B. Guillemet was a University of California, Riverside plant-pathology researcher involved in avocado rootstock evaluation and breeding. The Thomas plant patent identifies Guillemet as co-inventor and the Regents of the University of California as assignee. The evidence supports institutional ownership, not personal ownership by Guillemet or automatic ownership by later UCR personnel.
profiles.headquarters: University of California, Riverside, Riverside, California, United States
profiles.leadershipSummary: Guillemet is a historical program contributor rather than a current operator. The avocado rootstock program continued institutionally through other UCR researchers; no personal successor, current private company, or personal licensing contact is supported.
profiles.primaryContactName: UC Riverside Office of Technology Partnerships

webResearchSources.label: U.S. plant patent for Thomas naming the Regents as assignee
California Avocado Society research paper identifying Guillemet's UCR role
Official UC Riverside technology-commercialization office

profiles.webResearchNotes: The cited patent proves the ownership boundary for Thomas. Other rootstock records should still be checked individually because inventor lists and assignees can differ. No personal contact details or unsupported death/succession claims are included.

profiles.ownershipType: Family-company citrus breeder-member; company is patent and PBR rightsholder
profiles.ownershipSummary: The name resolves to William G. Roe II, also identified as Bill Roe. Citrus patent records name Bill Roe as inventor and GJH LLC as assignee, while an official South African gazette names GJH as applicant for the WG and C37 selections. Florida's current company record identifies William G. Roe II as a member of active GJH LLC. A recorded security interest does not establish an ownership transfer.
profiles.headquarters: GJH LLC, 118 West Central Avenue, Winter Haven, Florida 33880, United States
profiles.leadershipSummary: Florida's official record identifies Quentin J. Roe as managing member and William G. Roe II and Morgan H. Roe as members, indicating a family-company bench. Cultivar rights remain with GJH where the patent or PBR record names it, rather than with every Roe family member personally.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Official Florida records identify William G. Roe II as a member of GJH LLC. Patent and PBR sources independently establish GJH as rightsholder for cited selections; the company affiliation should not be generalized to any Roe record that names another holder.

webResearchSources.label: U.S. plant patent for WG32
Official South African gazette listing GJH citrus applications
Official Florida entity record for GJH LLC

profiles.webResearchNotes: The current GJH member record and historical patent title align. The 2017 Christensen Farms filing shown in patent events is a security interest, not evidence that Christensen Farms became owner. No public email or telephone was found, so only the official entity page is supplied as contact routing.

profiles.ownershipType: Former research-centre breeding director, now independent consultant; cultivar rights split by holder and jurisdiction
profiles.ownershipSummary: Daryl Somers formerly led applied genomics at Vineland Research and is later identified with Somers Consulting in Fenwick, Ontario. Canadian VDG001 records name Sunrise Greenhouses Ltd. as applicant and holder and show the right surrendered in 2023; the U.S. VDG002 patent names Vineland Research and Innovations Centre Inc. as assignee. Somers's present consulting work and later Sunrise collaborations do not inherit those historical grape rights.
profiles.headquarters: Current: Somers Consulting, 521 Welland Road, Fenwick, Ontario, Canada; historical employer: Vineland Research and Innovation Centre, Vineland Station, Ontario
profiles.leadershipSummary: At Vineland, Somers worked with breeder Rumen Conev and a broader institutional research team. His later consulting role is a separate operating period. Sunrise remains a recurring collaborator and record holder in some Canadian filings, but no evidence establishes it as Somers's employer or blanket owner of the VDG portfolio.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: A 2022 peer-reviewed publication and current public professional profile identify Somers with Somers Consulting. This supports current affiliation only and does not establish title to Vineland- or Sunrise-held VDG records.

webResearchSources.label: CFIA PBR record for VDG001
U.S. plant patent for VDG002
Peer-reviewed publication with current Somers Consulting affiliation

profiles.webResearchNotes: The exact identity is verified, but record splitting is required by cultivar, jurisdiction, status, and assignee. Historical Vineland contact details were not reused as current personal contact details. The public consulting address comes from a primary research publication.

profiles.ownershipType: Public-university professor emeritus and olive breeder; cited cultivar rights assigned to a private operator
profiles.ownershipSummary: The name resolves to Luis Rallo Romero, Professor Emeritus at the University of Cordoba and president of university spinout Pomologia S.L. He is one of six inventors on the I-series olive patents. The cited U.S. patent identifies Todolivo S.L. as current and original assignee following inventor assignments, so neither Rallo, the university, UCOLIVO, nor Pomologia should be substituted for Todolivo as rightsholder on those records.
profiles.headquarters: University of Cordoba, Rabanales Campus, Cordoba, Spain; Todolivo S.L. is a separate Cordoba-based commercial assignee
profiles.leadershipSummary: Rallo is an emeritus, late-career program leader. UCOLIVO's active bench includes Carlos Trapero Ramirez, Pedro Valverde Caballero, Diego Barranco Navero, Concepcion Munoz Diez, and other university researchers. The institutional team supports continuity while Todolivo holds the cited commercial patent rights.
profiles.primaryContactTitle: Professor Emeritus, University of Cordoba; President, Pomologia S.L.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: The official university profile and UCOLIVO site establish Rallo's academic and breeding-program affiliation. They do not establish university title to the cited I-series patents, which name Todolivo as assignee.

webResearchSources.label: Official University of Cordoba profile for Luis Rallo Romero
Official UCOLIVO breeding-program site and public contact
U.S. I77 plant patent naming Todolivo as assignee

profiles.webResearchNotes: The breeder, employer, spinout presidency, and assignee are distinct roles. Rallo's public-university and strategic-program position makes the profile non-actionable; outreach or ownership analysis for the I-series belongs at the record-level Todolivo rightsholder.

profiles.ownershipType: Active public-university olive breeder; cited cultivar rights assigned to a private operator
profiles.ownershipSummary: The name resolves to Carlos Trapero Ramirez, a permanent professor and active olive and pistachio breeder at the University of Cordoba. He is one of six inventors on the I-series olive patents, but the cited U.S. patent names Todolivo S.L. as current and original assignee. University employment and inventorship do not make Trapero personally, or UCOLIVO institutionally, the patent owner.
profiles.headquarters: University of Cordoba, Rabanales Campus, Cordoba, Spain; Todolivo S.L. is a separate Cordoba-based commercial assignee
profiles.leadershipSummary: Trapero is part of UCOLIVO's active generation and works alongside Pedro Valverde Caballero and the senior team of Luis Rallo Romero, Diego Barranco Navero, Concepcion Munoz Diez, and collaborators. The breeding bench is deep and institutionally supported.
profiles.primaryContactTitle: Official University of Cordoba olive breeding-program contact
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Official university materials establish Trapero's academic and UCOLIVO affiliation. The affiliation does not establish title to I-series patent rights assigned to Todolivo.

webResearchSources.label: Official University of Cordoba profile for Carlos Trapero
Official UCOLIVO breeding-program site and public contact
U.S. I77 plant patent naming Todolivo as assignee

profiles.webResearchNotes: The official profile URL uses a different slug but describes the exact Carlos Trapero Ramirez identity and role. This is a public-institution employee and strategic breeding operator, so the profile is non-actionable even though the cited protected varieties have a private assignee.

profiles.ownershipType: Active public-university olive breeder and postdoctoral researcher; cited cultivar rights assigned to a private operator
profiles.ownershipSummary: The name resolves to Pedro Valverde Caballero, a University of Cordoba olive breeder active since 2013 and co-responsible for public-private breeding lines. He is one of six inventors on the I-series olive patents, while the cited U.S. patent identifies Todolivo S.L. as current and original assignee. His current university role is not evidence of personal or university patent title.
profiles.headquarters: University of Cordoba, Rabanales Campus, Cordoba, Spain; Todolivo S.L. is a separate Cordoba-based commercial assignee
profiles.leadershipSummary: Valverde represents the active successor generation in UCOLIVO alongside Carlos Trapero Ramirez, supported by senior breeders Luis Rallo Romero, Diego Barranco Navero, Concepcion Munoz Diez, and other collaborators. The program has a visible scientific and public-private project bench.
profiles.primaryContactTitle: Postdoctoral researcher and olive breeder, University of Cordoba
profiles.candidateParentBasis: The official university profile establishes Valverde's employment and UCOLIVO breeding-program affiliation. That support is not title proof for I-series patent rights assigned to Todolivo.

webResearchSources.label: Official University of Cordoba profile for Pedro Valverde Caballero
Official UCOLIVO breeding-program site and public contact
U.S. I77 plant patent naming Todolivo as assignee

profiles.webResearchNotes: The source-backed university identity, public-private program role, and private patent assignee are kept separate. The active public-institution role makes this a non-actionable profile; titleholder analysis should route to Todolivo for the cited I-series records.

profiles.ownershipType: Historical Argentine grape breeder whose cultivar rights were assigned to multiple private entities
profiles.ownershipSummary: The name resolves to Angel Antonio Gargiulo, an Argentine grape breeder and inventor. U.S. records show cultivar-specific private ownership: 90-3437 and Black Globe were assigned through M. Caratan entities and later to ACMII California 6 LLC, while 90-3618 was assigned to L&M Nursery and other Gargiulo cultivars name additional Caratan-related assignees. No one later employer or assignee should receive the entire historical portfolio.
profiles.headquarters: Historical breeder location: Mendoza Province, Argentina; cited commercial assignees were based in California, United States
profiles.leadershipSummary: Gargiulo is a historical key-person breeder. Public patent sources do not establish a current employer, personal operating business, named scientific successor, or unified ownership bench. Continuity exists only through each cultivar's documented assignment chain.
profiles.candidateParent: M. Caratan, Inc. (historical repeated assignee only)
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Multiple patents support a recurring historical relationship between Gargiulo and M. Caratan entities, but they do not prove a single employer relationship or ownership of Gargiulo records assigned to L&M Nursery or other entities.

webResearchSources.label: U.S. plant patent and assignment history for 90-3437
U.S. plant patent and assignment history for Black Globe
Gargiulo U.S. patent portfolio showing multiple assignees

profiles.webResearchNotes: A record split is required because the aggregated name spans several assignees and later assignment chains. The cited 90-3437 and Black Globe U.S. patents are expired, which further limits their value as evidence of current exclusive rights. No unsupported INTA employment, current business contact, or blanket Caratan succession is asserted.
